g.title: argument
g.text: Mill on liberty of thought and discussion

g.title: cluster_5cf8a70e
g.text: Illegitimate and harmful suppression should be rej

g.title: claim_22cdead1
g.text: Silencing or suppressing an opinion assumes that the
suppressor is infallible in judging it false or harmful.

g.title: claim_2b523865
g.text: Human beings are fallible and cannot be certain that an

g.title: claim_2dc513d1
g.text: Society ought to permit freedom of opinion and freedom of

g.title: claim_342e91c6
g.text: A silenced opinion may be true, so suppressing it may block
the replacement of error with truth.

g.title: claim_380257f7
g.text: Suppressing discussion weakens understanding, moral courage,
and the capacity to correct error.

g.title: claim_3823cbad
g.text: Suppressing opinions harms mankind by obstructing truth,

g.title: claim_6e883b2f
g.text: Even when a silenced opinion is false, it may contain part
of the truth that prevailing opinion lacks.

g.title: claim_b34ee918
g.text: Even if the received opinion is true, free and open
contestation is needed for people to grasp it as a living
truth rather than a dead dogma.
